hero.subtitle: In the Amazon and in France, AKUU connects volunteers and local communities around concrete solidarity projects.
hero.image_alt: Peruvian Amazon, tropical forest and river

mission.title: Our mission
mission.text: AKUU works for sustainable, solidarity-based development that respects Amazonian populations, placing local cooperation, knowledge sharing and environmental protection at the heart of its actions.
mission.image_alt: Puerto Miguel village in the Amazon

featured_project.description: A cultural space in the heart of the Amazon to preserve and promote Kukama culture.

donation.text: Every donation directly contributes to the sustainable development of Puerto Miguel village and the preservation of Kukama culture.

association.who_text: AKUU is a French non-profit (loi 1901), founded on November 23, 2016. It carries out field missions in the Peruvian Amazon, mainly in Puerto Miguel, and runs awareness campaigns in the home countries of its volunteers.
association.quote: AKUU is a project I believe in with all my strength. AKUU reminds me of my connection with nature, with traditions. This word means the sharing of energies, the respect for the forest, its fauna, its flora...

durable.title: Sustainable development
durable.text: Promoting solutions that respect the environment and local resources.

solidarite.title: International solidarity
solidarite.text: Building bridges between cultures and fostering mutual aid between peoples.

culture.title: Respect for local cultures
culture.text: Valuing and preserving the ancestral knowledge of Kukama communities.

savoirs.title: Knowledge sharing
savoirs.text: Sharing knowledge to strengthen community autonomy.

environnement.title: Environmental protection
environnement.text: Taking concrete action to preserve the Amazon rainforest.

echanges.title: Intercultural exchanges
echanges.text: Fostering mutual understanding through sharing and meeting.

association.odd_subtitle: AKUU's actions are directly aligned with the United Nations 2030 Agenda. Each of our projects contributes to at least one SDG.
association.social_subtitle: Follow our news, photos and videos from the field and the life of the association.
association.social_subtitle_tiktok_only: Videos and posts from the field and the life of the association.

2019.title: Year of innovation

2020.description: The pandemic interrupts field missions. The team keeps the link remotely.

2021.description: Internal restructuring and strategic thinking on future projects.

2023.title: Museum launch

2024.title: Field missions & renovation
2024.description: Missions by Léa, Inès, Raphaël then Dylan and Paul. Building renovation in Puerto Miguel.

2025.title: Casa AKUU & official recognition
2025.description: Casa AKUU improvements. Grant from the Peruvian Ministry of Culture (19,960 soles) for Museo Shapishiko.

2026.title: Opening of the Shapishiko Museum
2026.description: Planned opening of the Shapishiko Museum in Puerto Miguel, a living space for Shipibo-Conibo culture and knowledge.

musee.hero_subtitle: Kukama culture, cosmovision and territory, in Puerto Miguel, where the Ucayali and Marañón meet to form the Amazon.
musee.description_intro: The Shapishiko Museum grew from the Las Mariposas artisans' initiative, with AKUU's support and the Puerto Miguel community. At the heart of Kukama territory on the Ucayali, this living space aims to uplift Indigenous culture while welcoming villagers and visitors.
description_objectives: Keep Kukama culture alive and visible
Raise awareness of Amazon forest conservation
Redirect part of existing tourism toward the village and strengthen local income
Create a lively place for exchange and transmission
musee.description_committee: A village management committee, 17 members, women and men aged 21 to 82, co-steers the project with AKUU. Progress is shared regularly at village assemblies.
musee.context_p1: Climate change and deforestation threaten the Amazon; protecting and valuing Indigenous territories helps address that. Our approach links culture, environment and the local economy.
musee.context_p2: Puerto Miguel has about 400 residents, mostly descendants of the Kukama-Kukamiria peoples. The language and ancestral knowledge are fragile; yet local initiatives, Las Mariposas, Radio Ucamara, village artists, show strong resilience and creativity.
musee.context_p3: The village sits in a tourist area (lodges on the Ucayali) where benefits rarely reach the community. The museum aims to become a draw within Puerto Miguel so value and attention flow back to residents.

mariposas.title: Las Mariposas
mariposas.text: Village artisans' association that sparked the project: chambira value chain and certification; the museum shop complements their sales points without competing with them.

nelvis.title: Nelvis Parades Pacaya
nelvis.text: Painter from the village; volunteers to pass on Shipibo and Kukama cultures to preserve knowledge and engage young people.

ecole_art.title: Puerto Miguel art school
ecole_art.text: Director: Casilda Pinches Sanchez. A place for training and creativity for young people in the village, linked to the museum project and local artists.
ecole_art.photo_alt: Puerto Miguel art school, Casilda Pinches Sanchez

ucamara.title: Radio Ucamara
ucamara.text: Territory and river advocacy, promotion of Kukama culture, research and education; a key partner for visibility and content.

chullachaqui.title: Chullachaqui Ecomuseum
chullachaqui.text: Near Iquitos; exchange on Amazonian cultures, training local guides and cooperation on museum content.

akuu.title: AKUU
akuu.text: French nonprofit supporting the project from the impact study, mobilising volunteers and funding, and gradually stepping back in favour of village governance.

musee.coupe_path_lead: The route runs from the interpretation centre on three levels (water, dense forest, canopy) to the cultural space via the Tsakarita walkway (Ikuachiru), then the shop and map of village artisans. The interactive section below shows each space in detail; scroll to explore and discover their Kukama names.
musee.governance_body:
Las Mariposas run the museum shop; part of the income helps maintain the site. AKUU aims to support rather than decide on behalf of residents.

The project also seeks recognition within Peru's museum network (process underway with iPeru and relevant institutions).
musee.teams_body: Successive AKUU volunteer teams work on site; civil works and local supervision are coordinated with the village, notably by Abraham Macuyama, an experienced construction lead from Puerto Miguel, with a team of about eleven skilled workers.
musee.visit_intro: Final arrangements will be set with the committee and local authorities.
musee.visit_duration: About thirty minutes per main interpretation room is planned; the full route (interpretation, cultural space, shop) may take around two hours depending on the group.
musee.visit_rates: Indicative pricing inspired by regional models: about 40 soles (roughly ten euros) for foreign visitors, free or preferential rates for local residents, subject to confirmation on site.
musee.visit_disclaimer: Non-binding information; please check with the museum before you travel.
musee.visit_photo_alt: Puerto Miguel village on the Ucayali River
musee.plans_intro: Site plan, architectural perspectives and the interactive ArchiCAD model. The section view of the spaces appears higher on this page. Drawings were developed by architect Maria Agustina Pistolesi, drawing inspiration notably from the Tree House Lodge in Puerto Miguel.
musee.gallery_subtitle: The Shapishiko Museum build, from design to work on the ground.

context.alt: Amazon forest and land around Puerto Miguel

governance.alt: Community life and decisions in the village

teams.alt: Build site and teams on the ground

musee.support_text: Contribute to the construction of the Shapishiko Museum and help us preserve Kukama culture for future generations.

coupe.title: Museum spaces
coupe.intro: The section drawing stays on screen while you scroll: each level is introduced in sequence. You can also tap a zone on the plan or the numbered buttons.
coupe.scroll_hint: Keep scrolling to explore all five spaces
coupe.alt: Architectural section of the Shapishiko Museum

kuruara.etage: Level 1, guardian of the second world
kuruara.description: The Kuruara is a spirit being who rules the submerged second world in Kukama cosmology. It rises from the depths, beautiful pink dolphin, shifting lights, water sounds. An outstretched hand on the ceiling wraps you in the heart of the floodable forest.

iwrati.etage: Level 2, guardian of the dense forest
iwrati.description: The Iw Rati Tuwa is a mischievous spirit roaming the forest depths, luring travellers only to lead them astray. Follow its trail and slip into the skin of the jaguar, the tapir and other dwellers of the dense forest.

tsumi.description: The Tsumi is the most powerful of Kukama healers, a spiritual traveller who can dive under water, rise into the air and take the shape of any animal. From the room at the top of the museum, look out over the forest canopy and follow the journeys of these sages whose art is fading today.

tsakarita.description: In Kukama, tsakarita means a shortcut, a direct link between two points. This walkway draws a straight line through time, plunging Kukama culture back into its pre-Columbian roots before reaching the exhibition space.

ikuachiru.description: In Kukama, ikuachiru is the place where knowledge lives. The last stop on the visit, both gallery and workshop: its walls tell stories, its tables host creation. Kukama art and craft are lived and passed on. A shop offers work by local artisans.

impact_terrain.label: Consultation and impact study (2019-2023)
items: Idea led by Las Mariposas; survey of Puerto Miguel residents
COVID pause and lessons; stronger partnerships
Village management committee formed (17 members, ages 21-82)
Partners: Radio Ucamara, Chullachaqui Ecomuseum, FAVER, Ministry of Culture (support for artistic content)

subvention_casa.label: Grant, Casa AKUU and artistic design
items: Grant from the Peruvian Ministry of Culture
Artistic design of the interpretation centre

production.label: 2026: fit-out, works and training
items: Purchase and production of works (wave 1, April-June 2026)
Works and fit-out: first floor of the interpretation centre, cultural space, sanitation (April-July 2026)
Initial training for the management committee (May-September 2026)
Cultural programme and first public workshops (from September 2026)
Completion of other levels, Tsakarita walkway, exterior (October 2026 - January 2027)

ouverture.label: Opening and consolidation (2026-2027)
items: Full official opening planned for June 2027

volontaires.hero_subtitle: Live a unique experience in the heart of the Peruvian Amazon

immersion.title: Real responsibilities
immersion.text: AKUU is a human-scale association. You're not a number: you respond to a need expressed directly by the community and own your mission from start to finish.

impact.title: Act for the Amazon
impact.text: The people who live in the Amazon forest are its greatest protectors. Working with them is the most concrete and honest way to contribute to its preservation.

skills.title: Learn, don't teach
skills.text: Kukama communities hold knowledge found nowhere else. Every former volunteer comes back with a different understanding of time, life and community.

community.title: A mission that matters
community.text: No boxes to tick, no photo opportunities. A useful presence, within a lasting project, alongside people who have chosen to welcome you.

step1.title: Application
step1.text: Send your application via our contact form.

step2.title: Interview
step2.text: Discussion with the team to validate your motivation and skills.

step4.title: Mission
step4.text: Departure for Puerto Miguel and immersion in projects.

civique.title: Civic service
civique.text: Via AIME ONG / France Volontaires. 6 to 12 months.

lea.quote: My time in Puerto Miguel changed how I see the world. Living alongside Kukama communities taught me what matters: sharing and respect for nature.

dylan.quote: Helping build the museum was the most rewarding experience of my life. You feel you're contributing to something lasting.

raphael.quote: Teaching English to children in Puerto Miguel opens a window on the world for them, and I learned so much from them in return.

volontaires.cta_text: Contact us to learn more about available missions.

soutien.label: Take action
soutien.hero_text1: All of AKUU's running costs (banking, logistics, website) are covered by our volunteers and close supporters.
soutien.simulator_note: 66% tax deduction for French residents (art. 200 CGI)
soutien.widget_badge: Monthly donation recommended · Cancel anytime · Automatic tax receipt
soutien.widget_secure: Secure payment by HelloAsso · Zero fees on your donation
soutien.other_subtitle: Beyond donating, join the AKUU adventure
soutien.adherer_text: From €10/year, become a member, attend general assemblies and receive our activity reports.
soutien.benevole_text: 3 to 6 months in Puerto Miguel or in France for AKUUVision. Construction, education, culture: a unique human experience.
soutien.partenaire_text: Company, institution, university: join us for a lasting and visible impact.

hydrama.hero_subtitle: Clean energy for isolated Amazonian communities
hydrama.suspended_notice: This project is currently suspended since COVID due to lack of funding. Its relaunch is being planned.
hydrama.what_text: HYDRAMA is a project to install a hydroelectric turbine in the Amazon river, to bring clean, renewable energy to the isolated community of Vista Alegre. The goal is to replace polluting generators with a sustainable energy source that respects the Amazonian environment.
hydrama.goal_1_text: Provide renewable electricity to Vista Alegre with no environmental impact.
hydrama.goal_2_text: Enable the community to manage its own energy source independently.
hydrama.goal_3_text: Develop a model transferable to other isolated Amazonian communities.

akuuvision.hero_subtitle: Raising awareness on climate and Amazonian issues, in France and Peru
akuuvision.what_text: AKUUVision is AKUU's awareness arm. Through participatory workshops, talks, and fun educational tools, we raise public awareness about climate issues and Amazonian communities. Our activities take place in France (schools, universities, companies) and in Puerto Miguel.
akuuvision.act_1_text: Collaborative workshops to understand climate change mechanisms.
akuuvision.act_2_text: School and university talks on Amazonian biodiversity.
akuuvision.act_3_text: Educational board game on environmental issues, created by AKUU.

cours_anglais.hero_subtitle: Teaching English in Puerto Miguel to open new opportunities
cours_anglais.suspended_notice: This project is currently suspended since COVID due to lack of funding. Its relaunch is being planned.
cours_anglais.what_text: Since 2017, AKUU provided free English classes to Puerto Miguel residents. English is essential for local ecotourism development and offers new professional opportunities for young community members. Classes reached over 60 students, children and adults, before being suspended by the pandemic.

yoann.bio: Co-founder of the association, Climate Fresk facilitator.

marlon.bio: From a small Amazonian village, Marlon is the essential link between AKUU and local communities.

footer.slogan: In the Amazon and in France, AKUU connects volunteers and local communities around concrete solidarity projects.
